commands.command: PYTHONPATH=src uv run python -m chronos.demo.cli publish-prepare
argv: PYTHONPATH=src uv run python -m chronos.demo.cli publish-prepare
commands.observed_behavior: Built and validated a prepared PublicationAttempt: bound HUD deploy primitive (COMMANDS.json:hud-deploy), authorized target (account 'mongodb-sales-aggregation-engine' registry), passing ReleaseProof, sealed candidate, offline-verified hardened v2 grader, idempotency key over proof digest + target. Registry upload deliberately deferred; no environment published.

Reproduced the Plan 005 release-verification harness (_DockerVerifierRunner) on a freshly-materialized hardened v2 in real mongo:7.0 containers: witness exploit reward 0.0 (killed), all three controls reward 1.0 (preserved), grader_v2_digest matches the sealed ReleaseProof. Closes the served-runtime kill-parity gap. No HUD upload performed.

Builds the actual deployable image and runs env.py's grader. v6 out-of-process grader: witness 0.0, three controls 1.0, in-process exit-code hijack 0.0, in-process MONKEYPATCH 0.0, and root tampering of the digest-pinned grader files (overwrite grade.py/reference, shadow stdlib) all 0.0. deploy_form_pass=true. The candidate runs only in throwaway isolated subprocesses (python3 -I run_solution.py); the verdict process (grade.py) never imports candidate code.

commands.command: uv run hud deploy <assembled v2 context>  (HUD_API_KEY from root .env)

Server-side AWS CodeBuild + ECR push. Non-destructive: builds total 4 (v4 latest; v1-v3 retained). Verified via read-only GET /v2/registry. See docs/plans/evidence/006/publish-receipt.json.

checks.name: Demo readiness pack validation
checks.evidence: Plan 006 now validates a digest-checked demo readiness pack for presentation/acceptance modes without probing live systems or reading secrets. The pack requires evidence-backed checks for HUD auth, Modal auth, model gateway auth, network, quota, source trace, ForkPoint, prior Witness, replay entrypoint, ProofSet, ReleaseProof, release candidate, publication attempt or expected block, and local artifact paths. Blocked packs require explicit blocked/expected-block reasons; passing packs cannot include blockers; secret-like content is rejected.

checks.name: Core metrics validation

Each metric may be measured with evidence_ref or absent as not-measured/not-applicable with reason, preserving honest pre-Gate-4 reporting.

checks.name: Publication: bound, prepared, and PUBLISHED v5 (live)
checks.evidence: hud deploy published the hardened env as mongodb-sales-aggregation-engine version 5 (registry b3409057, build 0290bbb9 SUCCEEDED, team hud-hacks; docs/plans/evidence/006/publish-receipt.json), verified via read-only GET /v2/registry. Non-destructive: v4 latest, v1-v3 retained (total 4). publish-record wrote a validated 'published' PublicationAttempt (published_environment_ref hud:registry/b3409057@v5, trusted publication evidence ref, passing ReleaseProof gate, no error class). INTERFACES.md row 17 = verified (published v4). COMMANDS.json:hud-deploy stays not-applicable so CI cannot re-deploy. SCOPE: the published env defeats the recorded reward hack + tested variants only; a verifier-isolation STOP is recorded (see the isolation check).

checks.name: Post-publish bug hunt: v3 grader bypass found and fixed (v4)
checks.evidence: A parallel subagent bug-hunt found a GENUINE bypass in the published v3 grader: because the agent runs as root, a force-pass conftest.py planted directly in /tests/task_assets defeated it (PYTEST_DISABLE_PLUGIN_AUTOLOAD does not disable conftest discovery; the v3 digest gate only covered test_outputs.py). Verified with a real-container repro. Fixed in env.py (--noconftest, -p no:cacheprovider, PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE, and a full /tests tree allow-list); the deploy-proof now loads GRADE_CMD verbatim from env.py and tests the conftest-in-/tests and sitecustomize variants. Re-proven (v2-deploy-proof.json) and re-deployed as v4.

checks.name: Verifier-isolation in-process bypass FIXED out-of-process (v6); hardcoding residual recorded
checks.evidence: The in-process bypass class (exit-code hijack + monkeypatch) is fixed by the v6 out-of-process grader (artifacts/chronos/demo/publish/v2-env/grader/grade.py + run_solution.py + reference_query.py): env.py runs `python3 -I /tests/grader/grade.py`, which executes the candidate only in throwaway isolated subprocesses and compares its outputs to the trusted reference solution; the verdict process never imports candidate code. Re-proven in v2-deploy-proof.json (exit_code_hijack_blocked, monkeypatch_blocked, root_grader_tamper_blocked, controls preserved). This follows the harden-v0/Harbor reward-by-separate-process convention (see the harden-v0 investigation). The LIVE published env is still v5 (XML grader, monkeypatch-vulnerable) pending a v6 deploy decision. Remaining residual: answer-hardcoding by a root candidate, which needs randomised/hidden test data or a non-root candidate / SEPARATE verifier environment (env/Plan-005 hardening). Recorded in artifacts/chronos/demo/publish/v2-security-limitations.json.

2026-06-21T18:10:00Z — Implemented the verifier-isolation fix following the harden-v0/Harbor convention. harden-v0 delegates grading to Harbor, which runs the verifier as a SEPARATE process writing a reward FILE (never the candidate's exit code), optionally in a SEPARATE verifier environment / as a non-agent user. Adopted that out-of-process pattern as the v6 grader: env.py runs `python3 -I /tests/grader/grade.py`, which runs the candidate and the trusted reference solution in throwaway isolated subprocesses and compares outputs — the verdict process never imports candidate code. Re-proven (v2-deploy-proof.json): witness/exit-hijack/monkeypatch/root-grader-tamper all blocked, controls preserved. Committed and proven; the live env is v5 pending a v6 deploy decision.
